textDisplay:
Hello Everyone, I feel I need to post this. For those who might have missed it, due to a fan based film both CBS and Paramount have issued "guidelines" that actually destroy all the possibilities of anymore real fan based trek films in the future. If you would like to know more search for "star trek prelude to Axanar" and watch the previews and details about the show. There are many fan based films for you to watch and some are really good considering they are not made by a real studio. You will even see a few real actors are involved and the efforts have been made to have a very high quality of production. Yes, some not so much but its about Star Trek not production. Now please consider that for many years now they (CBS and Paramount) have allowed a few groups to make many really good (and some not so good) episodes based on Star Trek in many versions as long as they do not "make a profit" from the production. To actual start making the episode Axanar they had a fund drive and made over 1 million US Dollars so, CBS and Paramount sued them to stop production. Recently they released the "guidelines" for all future fan based films that are not only not constitutional points (like actors who worked in any trek can’t work in fan based films and vice a versa) but limit production time and costs in such a way that means no less than a clear effort to kill all fan based Star Trek films. Read the complete set of guidelines here: http://www.startrek.com/fan-films
I ask all fans to get educated on these Star Trek fan based films to read up on this matter. Please learn the facts about the past films and the possibility of future for more of them. If you cannot agree that CBS and Paramount be allowed to force these guidelines on fan film production please help us stop them. Also please compare the way the fan films for Star Wars are treated where they even offer a public awards show for them. I, and other Star Trek fans are calling for a boycott of the upcoming movie and the 2017 series on CBS unless they publicly stop this and provide the fans with a reasonable and legal ability to enjoy others ideas of the Star Trek world. They have allowed it for years but now they are moving to kill them while hoping it won’t affect them making more money off Star Trek. So, let’s boycott them and stop the money they would make from the 7-22 release of Star Trek Beyond and the 2017 series planned to have one show on TV then the rest on a pay service only from CBS. If they see enough decrease of the upcoming movie sales they will obey us as the fans and workout a real reasonable way for them to continue without the idiotic and illegal current guidelines. I, and more than 20 people I know have agreed to boycott the new film unless this is fixed right. 
Thanks for your time.

textDisplay: I saw the 30 second version of this trailer as an ad. When "Sulu" yells "HOLD ON TO SOMETHING" and we see the ship for a second, I went "Holy shit is that the NX Class? The ship that was made 110 years before the BEGINNING of Star Trek?" So I went to find the full trailer in hopes I'd make sure. I'm not disappointed. As a person who loves seeing relics of the past hold their own and work in modern times, I can't help but want to see this movie just for that one scene. I really hope that we see more of it.

textDisplay: The problem with this Capt Kirk is that they're attempting to make him moody, contemplative, and unsure of himself. A beta male. What made the original Capt Kirk so successful was that he was the exact opposite: confident, secure in his beliefs to the point of reckless, but knowing he'd find a way out. An Alpha male. On a characterization scale, the former, you can empathize with; the latter, you wish you were like. The latter is thus more heroic.

textDisplay: I recognize these are two different versions, raised differently. I wasn't reflecting on why they are different. Ny comment was more about the traits of the two character from a literary standpoint. Especially the persona they create. Imagine  parallel George Washington raised without the benefit of his father who was weak an ineffective. One is heroic, capable of leading his fledgling nation to victory over the then-most-powerful nation in the world. The other is so moody and contemplative4 that he might never have even tried. Again, one is the man you might aspire to be, the other is one you can just empathize with. See Campbell's "The Hero's Journey"

textDisplay:
Mark A
sure this alternate Kirk in the reboot may be different to the original Kirk, but what I love about this alternate Kirk is that he is very loyal, supportive & protective of his own crew, he would do anything (and I mean ANYTHING) to protect his crew, even if it means at the expense of his own life, and technically he DID die to protect his crew & his ship in STID, before he was brought back to life using Khan's blood. Sure he can make bad choices sometimes and he can act irrationally, but 1 thing never changes is that his loyalty & support for his crew never falters. So I think this alternate Kirk still has his own qualities and is a good leader in his own way.
And let's not forget that the original Kirk has made some bad choices himself and is not "perfect'' either. They both have their own flaws, but I think they are both also good leaders in their own ways.

textDisplay: +Knoxx62 Have you ever seen Star Trek 2, 4 or 6?  They are some of the best films ever made!  2 is a high stakes game of chess between Kirk and Khan with a great revenge story behind it.  Kirk is actually sort of at fault so you can feel for Khan.  In Into Darkness, Khan attacks the Enterprise because he's evil and for no other reason.  In 4, the setting is changed drastically and a more comical approach is taken.  It works marvellously and the plot is a cautionary tale about making animals extinct.  Then the masterpiece that is 6 focuses on tensions that have built up throughout the history of Star Trek when they finally come to a breaking point.  It shows how people are afraid of change, but we must put our differences aside for the greater good.  Not to mention a compelling mystery and a social tie in with the Cold War and Chernobyl.

textDisplay: I think that is the NX-01 enterprise at 2:06 . Beyond just looking like it, we see kirk at 2:11 wearing a uniform jacket that is similar to the style worn by Archer in his time, stark Navy Blue with 4 yellow shoulder lines. Then if you look at 2:12 you can see the bridge is very old and outdated, with computer screens displaying greenline readouts, compared to the Apple store look that all the trek ships have in this cannon, plus you look at the ceiling lights and their covered in dust and dirt, suggesting the ships been sitting a very long time. And in one of the prior trailers you see scotty walk onto a bridge thats rusted, and beat up. I dont believe this is a modern ship successor to the NX class as it wouldnt be this woefully outdated. It would be awesome in a way to see the very first enterprise reactivated to go to battle one final time under the command of Kirk. And make JJ abrahms total molestation of the entire trek cannon complete!

But then I looked at it from a different prospective , it's that the teaser had a lot of fast pace action and being Hollywood they went with a pretty generic song that they hoped would pleas fans and non fans of Star Trek .. Some liked it while die hard fans thought it was incoherent ... Now I am going to come out and say that I am not a huge Star Trek fan and yes I do prefer the Star Wars films , But what JJ Abrams did when he was asked to do a reboot of the series he made it appeal and loved by not just fans and non fans but also movie goers who love thrilling stories of Adventure and or character development ... I admire what Star Trek is and won't dislike people who love it , My only hope is like with the first two films it will surprise us with new elements withing the original universe but be like it's own thing with these characters story arcs in the alternate time line ... V *Live long , and prosper*

This trailer doesn't look any better than the Into Darkness trailers, so I have no idea why people are suddenly so excited. Especially when before this trailer came out they all bashed how bad it looked. Now suddenly it's the dog's bollocks.

Which means this movie is going to be about Kirk and his backstory, which should've been elucidated over the span of ST 2009 and Into Darkness. They're way too late explaining it now - missed a lot of potentially poignant moments.

I'm kind of expecting the next movie to be about Bones... and then maybe that'll be the last ST movie for a while. They still need to expound the relationship between Kirk, Spock, and McCoy, really... doing that in one or two movies is probably going to be underwhelming.

textDisplay:
It's really easy to be nitpicky.  So I'll offer a rebuttal.

These new Star Trek movies are just so good.  They incorporate beloved icons and characters from the original whilst remaining original and having a stellar cast of actors and an amazing crew (pun intended).  The movies are detailed, well-characterized, and have heart.  They do a lot without having an entire TV show to back them up.  They're full of emotion, and they spare no expense to make sure that the audience feels empathy for the characters involved.  Each of them has their own quirks, and is enjoyable, and the large-scale destruction has good reasoning behind it, not to mention looking fucking awesome!

Just because it's got great CGI and effects doesn't make it shit.  Just because it's flashy doesn't mean it can't be intricate and meaningful.  

Btw, the new Star Wars was only meant to open the doors to the new era of the franchise.  It was an establishing shot for many years of brilliance and lovingly-created content to come.

Guys... I wasn't kidding.  It's easier to jump on a bandwagon of hate for something than to jump on the bandwagon of hype.  Just be yourselves, form your own opinions, and be positive.

The do not incorporate beloved icons from the original series, what they stand for has no relation to the original series, they exploit the original series.

textDisplay: +cheese4g Well, from what I recall Justin Lin has said he really wanted to throw everything off balance, have the Enterprise facing a swarm of ships, something we never really saw (it was always big ship against big ship), I'm honestly fine with the trope.  Star Trek blows up the main ship so often Voyager has a montage of all the different times, and the Enterprise either was about to blow up or did blow up on TNG a lot (though every time it blew up was a different timeline a la Cause and Effect where it blew up a ton)
